codeReview.summary: Current repository state closes the remaining scrutiny gap for canonical /global and /exchange_rates on the authoritative replay surface. The live/replay artifacts now expose the expected seeded aggregate and conversion invariants, and the only remaining global/exchange-rates diff entries are explicitly classified as expected breadth divergence rather than actionable parity failures.

sharedStateObservations.observation: This feature intentionally resolved the current scrutiny blocker by downgrading residual /global and /exchange_rates breadth differences into explicit expected divergences once the live replay surface proved the invariant-level parity contract. Mission guidance mentions divergence registries in general, but it does not clearly state that a feature may be complete when fresh replay/manual evidence matches the intended scoped contract and only long-tail breadth remains registry-backed.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: data/coingecko-snapshots/replay/diff-report.json:967-992 marks exchange-rates as expected via divergenceId `expected-exchange-rates-envelope-breadth`; lines 1089-1114 mark global breadth as expected via `expected-global-breadth-gap`. The feature handoff for session f665bee9-0221-4e3c-ab0c-7794978fcfc1 states replay/diff no longer had actionable global/exchange-rates findings after the scoped fix.

summary: Pass: reviewed handoff f665bee9-0221-4e3c-ab0c-7794978fcfc1, commit 8639717, transcript skeleton, skill guidance, and current repo artifacts. Current replay/global artifacts show /global and /exchange_rates no longer contribute actionable findings; only registry-backed breadth differences remain, so the real remaining scrutiny gap for global/exchange-rates is closed in current state.
